chapter_question: Why is total turning stable under deformation?
inspection_targets: A loop deforms while its tangent degree remains stable.
The tangent or normal map winds around S1.
Positive and negative curvature intervals preserve total degree.
The invariant changes only through forbidden singular events.

source_span.focus: Gives a first visual proof through total curvature of loops and deformed surfaces.
source_span.label: Chapter 17
source_span.title: First (Heuristic) Proof of the Global Gauss-Bonnet Theorem
topics: Heuristic proof of the turning theorem
Total curvature of a deformed sphere
How local turning data becomes global topology
